title: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

defines.name: LoRA
defines.definition: A parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that freezes pretrained model weights and injects trainable low-rank decomposition matrices into Transformer layers to adapt large language models.

problem: Full fine-tuning of large pretrained language models is too expensive in storage, memory, and deployment cost, while existing parameter-efficient methods often add latency or reduce quality.
